name: Hampton Hawes
abstract: Hampton Barnett Hawes Jr. (November 13, 1928 – May 22, 1977) was an American jazz pianist.

concerts.label: Newport Jazz Festival: Stanley Turrentine, John Klemmer, and Hampton Hawes

about_quotes.speaker: Chico Hamilton
about_quotes.evidence: And the piano player was Hampton Hawes and Jody Williams... Floyd had a 15 piece band... So I then got a gig, got this gig... I'm playing on a gig... Anyway Floyd had a 15 piece band... And we played, we did gigs around LA every now and then
about_quotes.doc_title: Chico Hamilton — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Patricia Willard
about_quotes.evidence: Yeah, we used to play the Sunday sessions at the Toddle House, Wardell and Sonny Criss and Hampton Hawes and Chuck Thompson and Roy Porter and all of us

about_quotes.speaker: Jackie McLean
about_quotes.evidence: They wanted me to come out and play with Hampton Hawes... I was out there for about three weeks with Hampton. We played three different clubs.
about_quotes.doc_title: Jackie McLean — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Howard McGhee
about_quotes.evidence: we went into a place called the Hi-De-Ho Club. It was just me and Bird and Hampton Hawes, and Dingbod and Roy Porter.
